description: Senate at Odds Over Reconstruction Bills Weeks Plan Would Put After the War Power in Hands of Congress Over mans Idea to Give It to President By RICHARD BARRY TANARUS KFQEE this country can have a re O construction programme it must settle the question of who is to handle it Congress or the President Recently I outlined in Tjib Sex the programme of Senator Weeks who had just introduced a resolution calling for the appointment by Congress of a committee on reconstruc tion A few days later Senator Overman introduced a resolution that covered prac tically the same ground in all but one vital essential The Overman bill asked for a commission on reconstruction to be appointed by the President There today lies Americas greatest problem next to the imperative task of winning the war The question is Will Congress continue after the war on the line it has willingly followed for seven teen months delegating much of its legis lative function to the Chief Executive or will it rise now and reassert itself re claiming its constitutional powers even if tliis must be done in the midst of war and at therisk of confusing m many minds the
